checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet (2014 comparative); 10-K income statement FY2014 (0001193125-15-085312)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Revenues=85828000 (2010 annual) vs Revenues=138726000 (2011 annual), a 62% increase

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 comparative income statement and cash flow statement
10-K FY2014 income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfServices=75433000 / Revenues=138726000 (2011 annual, ~54.4% cost ratio) vs CostOfServices=114581000 / Revenues=182644000 (2014 annual, ~62.7% cost ratio)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=61050000 (2011-12-31) with Revenues=138726000 (2011 annual) vs Liabilities=24516000 (2014-12-31) with Revenues=182644000 (2014 annual)
evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet 2014-12-31 comparative; 10-K FY2011/2014 income statements

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=793000 (2011-12-31) vs 583000 (2014-12-31) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent moved from 30635000 (2011-12-31) to 25941000 (2014-12-31)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 8-K filed 2013-12-16 (accession 0001193125-13-474701) contains recast historical financial statement data spanning FY2009-2012 (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts 2009-2013, Assets 2011, Liabilities 2011, Revenues 2010, NetIncomeLoss 2010, StockholdersEquity 2009, etc.), filed the same day as an S-3 shelf registration
evidence.location: 8-K filed 2013-12-16, same-day S-3 filing in chronology

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, Assets, Liabilities, cash flow statement items and receivables/goodwill from 2008 through Q3 2015, across 10-K, 10-Q and 8-K filings

top_signals: Large, recurring, largely unexplained gaps between OperatingIncomeLoss and NetIncomeLoss in 2012 (annual), Q3 2014 and Q2 2015 (tens of millions of dollars), suggesting significant non-operating/below-the-line items (e.g., impairments, tax valuation allowance swings, or gains/losses on divestitures) that materially move reported net income without proportional operating cause

An 8-K filed 2013-12-16 recast several years of historical financial statement line items (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow) concurrently with a same-day S-3 shelf registration, indicating a reclassification (e.g., discontinued operations) that complicates period-over-period comparability

Total liabilities declined substantially (Liabilities=61050000 at 2011-12-31 to Liabilities=24516000 at 2014-12-31) even as revenue nearly doubled (Revenues=85828000 in 2010 to Revenues=182644000 in 2014), which is atypical for a scaling business and warrants review of accrual/reserve completeness

Goodwill grew far faster than revenue in 2011 (Goodwill roughly doubled from 35337000 to 82644000 versus 62% revenue growth) followed by a partial write-down in 2012, coincident with a large net loss (NetIncomeLoss=-35196000) far exceeding the operating loss (OperatingIncomeLoss=-17243000) for that year
